preamble: The raising of mid-front [e] to high-front [i] occurs in unstressed syllables across many languages. In Russian, the process is called 'ikanie': unstressed /e/ is realized as [ɪ] (close to [i]) in pre-tonic and post-tonic positions. The shift is also common in rapid speech and as part of historical vowel shifts.

examples.note: Russian ikanie: unstressed /e/ → [ɪ] in pre-tonic position. The first syllable of 'reka' (river) is realized as [rʲɪ-], not [rʲe-].
Short Latin /e/ and /i/ merged in many Romance languages to produce /i/ in certain unstressed positions.
